Accidents are very common today. There are many accidents that happen every day. These accidents cause traffic congestion and can take hours clear. When vehicles were first introduced not all could afford it. Further was not considered worthy investment. Only few people used it. Over time the demand and usefulness vehicles began show. This caused huge demand which resulted the world live today. The moment step out our houses see roads. Vehicles have become important that use more than half the land for roads. This itself shows the importance place vehicles and the affect has our daily lives. All use vehicles travel and crucial requirement. This requirement has been increasing while the number vehicles has increased. The increase has resulted congestion the road. Congestion not the only reason for accidents, there are many. Here are the main reasons for vehicular accidents occur.
The main reasons for accidents are when the driver gets distracted. This results the driver ignoring the road and what front. Usually the main distractions are phone calls, text messages, smartphone usage, looking somewhere else and meddling with the radio. These accidents are dangerous well. The driver may hit bystander.

Somnath Temple one the twelve jyotirlinga India. Hindu worship Lord Shiva the form linga and all the Shiva Linga jyotirlinga are famous swayambhu. Swayambhu means that manifest its own i.e. not made man. These lingas are self-existing and appear suddenly mysteriously without any human efforts. When the saints holy men see them they install them temple. Each the twelve jyotirlinga, take the name different manifestation Shiva. These twelve jyotirlinga are considered very holy for the Shiva devotees. All the Shiva devotees visit these jyotirlinga one one all are located different locations and can’t visited all once. believed that just visit the temple very auspicious and all bad deeds and sins are washed away the feet Lord Shiva.

Towards the end the past two weeks Ardern was getting visibly frustrated with both media questioning and Opposition attacks. part this will stem from this being the first time her Government has been hauled over the coals for significant length time. But doubt lot her annoyance will come from the fact that most the problems she's been having deal with aren't ones that she's been responsible for, barring her poor handling the Russia issue.
The rough patch set continue too. With the Select Committee submissions soon heard the Electoral Integrity Amendment Bill, there will stream negative headlines about the Government pushing that Bill through, well the Green Party's support for it. There's also lingering questions around Winston Peters' infatuation with Putin's Russia.
shouldn't escape anyone's notice that New Zealand First, who are struggling badly polls, have been the source three the issues that have dogged the Government the past two weeks. Shane Jones' comments are perhaps the most interesting this regard, they point towards New Zealand First taking much more vocal stand issues that might not always sit well with the and requirements occupying the Government benches.
The good news for Labour that with Easter fast approaching, and beyond that the beginning pre-Budget announcements, the Government does have opportunity start setting the news agenda rather than reacting it.

Health authorities New Zealand said that about 200 passengers the Dawn Princess ship became infected with the norovirus.
The ship was scheduled leave for Australia Monday. The last time there was norovirus outbreak the ship was back 2012.
According Yahoo, health officials conducted series tests, and they confirmed that the illness was norovirus, but the outbreak does seem going away.
The norovirus usually lasts for one three days, and those infected may experience stomach pains, vomiting, diarrhea and nausea.
Princess Cruises released statement saying that those who were infected were isolated their cabins. They remained there until they were considered not contagious. The statement continued say that crew members disinfected door handles, railings, elevator buttons and forth.
The cruise operator also said that passengers were encouraged wash their hands properly and that they should use sanitizing gels.
About month ago, another cruise ship, the Crown Princess, had outbreak the norovirus. that incident more than 150 crew members and passengers came down with the norovirus. That ship was also operated Princess Cruises.

But not just Indonesia’s actions that are bringing these tensions the fore. number other countries have demonstrated renewed enthusiasm for executing drug offenders over the past year: Iran has escalated drug related hangings, Pakistan has resumed executions and Oman has proposed introduce capital punishment for drug crimes. protest end the use the death penalty for drugs will take place outside headquarters Vienna during the Commission meeting.
The statement from the INCB body independent experts that monitors the the international drug control treaties reinforces similar call the group made one year ago, which added its voice those other authorities, including the the High Commissioner for Human Rights and the Office Drugs and Crime (UNODC), who have called for ending the death penalty for drug offences.
Ironically, although the UNODC has publicly spoken out against the death penalty for drug offences, the agency has been linked funding aggressive operations several death penalty States, and number donor governments have publicly acknowledged the link between UNODC funding and death sentences. recent years the United Kingdom, Denmark and Ireland all resolved end funding UNODC programmes certain countries while the death penalty still enforced.

William: Hello, I'm William Kremer and this The English Speak.
Wang Fei: Hmm… Tuesday?
William: Yes, it's Tuesday, but it's not just any Tuesday. Today World Press Freedom Day. This the day that the United Nations has chosen highlight the importance free press around the world.
Wang Fei: free press. So, newspapers that are free write anything they think the public need know and news that can report anything?
William: Yes they can report anything, including things that look bad for the government! Scandals.
Wang Fei: scandal, something very bad that damages someone's reputation.
William: Exactly. And one the most famous scandals from American history the Watergate scandal the early 1970s.
Wang Fei: Watergate… that was why President Nixon had resign wasn't it?
William: Exactly, yes, because was shown have lied the American people and basically obstructed the course justice. The whole thing started when five men were arrested for breaking into office block called Watergate. This was where the Democratic Party had their headquarters. Later on, the men were linked the campaign re-elect President Nixon, who was the Republican Party.
Wang Fei: Well, this very interesting but what has got with The English Speak, William?

Jessie coming "Bang Bang" collaborator Ariana Grande's defense against those nasty diva rumors. Recently Ari has been plagued with claims that she rude fans, has long list ridiculous demands, and won't let photographers shoot her from the right side her face—and Jessie wants you know that those stories are far from the truth.
While appearance NYC, Jessie spoke with Weekly about working with Ariana and how she thinks some people mistake her passion for diva behavior.
"I've met Ariana," said Jessie, "And there's very thin line between 'diva' and 'survival.' And lot people can't [differentiate that]—and I've had that. I've had the diva stuff. And it's when people can't cope with how passionate you are, and how much you care."
Clearly Ariana's passion working for her, because she has been dominating the music charts.
"She's super talented, she's very young, and she's very sure what she wants," Jessie continued. think she's wonderful. She's great."

President Donald Trump returned Washington, Monday for very important event.
No, this day, Trump greeted thousands children. They had come the White House for the annual Easter Egg Roll.
The event has been U.S. tradition for more than century. the late 1800s, local children gathered the hill beside the president’s house play game with their colored eggs following the Easter holiday.
The White House Easter Egg Roll was special interest this year. The Trump was very late organize it. Some people who hoped attend the event were even worried that would canceled.
Political observers said the Easter Egg Roll would show how well Trump’s could plan and operate complex public relations event.
Talk about the event also brought attention the first lady, whose office usually plans the White House Easter Egg Roll. First Lady Melania Trump has not yet moved Washington, DC. She says she will remain New York City until her son finishes the school year.
So, how did the White House Easter Egg Roll this year?
Just fine, early reports say.
About 21,000 children and their families received tickets this year’s event. They came small groups the lawn near the president’s house. They played soccer, tossed bean bags and, course, pushed hard-boiled eggs down the hill.
The event was smaller than previous years. Last year, for example, about 36,000 people attended.

you've been around foodies for any length time, the subject coconut oil has probably come up.
Coconut oil saturated fat that most people unfortunately believe the cause health problems such heart disease, cancer, and weight gain. This false accusation has made into low-fat society Can't Believe It's Not Butter” huggers.
But unlike the popular fats like canola oil, vegetable oil, and wannabe butter spreads, saturated fats (animal fats and tropical oils like coconut oil and palm oil) are actually the healthiest fats you can eat and use variety ways.
Here are reasons eat coconut oil, plus ways use it. (In case you're counting, that adds 10.) Including recipes!
From the very beginning life, saturated fats (coconut oil 92% saturated) are beneficial. They contain lauric acid, the same fatty acid found breast milk, which makes them essential for brain development, from newborns those suffering from Alzheimer's.
Low-fat dieters may appalled the thought, but eating coconut oil will actually help you maintain healthy weight and boost your metabolism. Saturated fats are directly absorbed into your body for quick energy. They boost metabolism and stimulate weight loss.
According several doctors, including Dr. Bruce Fife, coconut oil can help protect you from heart disease, cancer, and other degenerative conditions.

Margot Shetterly spoke FSU.
FAYETTEVILLE, (WTVD) The author "Hidden Figures" shared some inspiration Tuesday with students Fayetteville State University.
Margot Shetterly's book, "Hidden Figures" was the basis for the Academy movie about black female working for NASA.
was packed house the Seabrook Auditorium. Shetterly's speech was part the chancellor's distinguished speaker series. Many wanted hear from the author who introduced the untold story four trailblazers for African American women Dorothy Vaughn, Mary Jackson, Katherine Johnson and Christine Darden, who originally from North Carolina.
Shetterly walked the audience through what took write such profound book. She spent countless hours research dedicated capturing the stories these pioneer women who helped launch astronaut John Glenn into outer space.
Shetterly said she wrote the book because she didn't want their contributions continue going unnoticed.
